delegation-sets.description: Whether to create Route53 delegation sets
delegation-sets.name: create

delegation-sets.description: Map of Route53 delegation set parameters
delegation-sets.name: delegation_sets

resolver-endpoint.description: Name to use on security group created
resolver-endpoint.name: security_group_name

resolver-endpoint.description: Security group tcp/udp on port 53 ingress rules to add to the security group created
resolver-endpoint.name: security_group_ingress_rules
resolver-endpoint.type:
map(object({
    name = optional(string)

    cidr_ipv4                    = optional(string)
    cidr_ipv6                    = optional(string)
    description                  = optional(string)
    prefix_list_id               = optional(string)
    referenced_security_group_id = optional(string)
    tags                         = optional(map(string), {})
  }))

resolver-endpoint.description: Security group tcp/udp on port 53 egress rules to add to the security group created
resolver-endpoint.name: security_group_egress_rules
resolver-endpoint.type:
map(object({
    name = optional(string)

    cidr_ipv4                    = optional(string)
    cidr_ipv6                    = optional(string)
    description                  = optional(string)
    prefix_list_id               = optional(string)
    referenced_security_group_id = optional(string)
    tags                         = optional(map(string), {})
  }))

resolver-endpoint.description: Determines whether resources will be created (affects all resources)
resolver-endpoint.name: create

resolver-endpoint.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
resolver-endpoint.name: region

resolver-endpoint.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
resolver-endpoint.name: tags

resolver-endpoint.description: Subnets and IP addresses in your VPC that you want DNS queries to pass through on the way from your VPCs to your network (for outbound endpoints) or on the way from your network to your VPCs (for inbound endpoints)
resolver-endpoint.name: ip_address

resolver-endpoint.description: Determines if a security group is created
resolver-endpoint.name: create_security_group

resolver-endpoint.description: A map of additional tags to add to the security group created
resolver-endpoint.name: security_group_tags

resolver-endpoint.description: Friendly name of the Route 53 Resolver endpoint
resolver-endpoint.name: name

resolver-endpoint.description: Description of the security group created
resolver-endpoint.name: security_group_description

resolver-endpoint.description: The VPC ID where the security group will be created
resolver-endpoint.name: vpc_id

resolver-endpoint.description: Protocols you want to use for the Route 53 Resolver endpoint. Valid values are `DoH`, `Do53`, or `DoH-FIPS`
resolver-endpoint.name: protocols

resolver-endpoint.description: ID of one or more security groups that you want to use to control access to this VPC
resolver-endpoint.name: security_group_ids

resolver-endpoint.description: Determines whether the security group name (`security_group_name`) is used as a prefix
resolver-endpoint.name: security_group_use_name_prefix

resolver-endpoint.description: A map of Route 53 Resolver rules & associations to create
resolver-endpoint.name: rules
resolver-endpoint.type:
map(object({
    # Rule
    domain_name = string
    name        = optional(string)
    rule_type   = string
    tags        = optional(map(string), {})
    target_ip = optional(list(object({
      ip       = string
      ipv6     = optional(string)
      port     = optional(number)
      protocol = optional(string)
    })))

    # Association
    vpc_id = optional(string)
  }))

resolver-endpoint.description: Direction of DNS queries to or from the Route 53 Resolver endpoint. Valid values are `INBOUND` (resolver forwards DNS queries to the DNS service for a VPC from your network or another VPC) or `OUTBOUND` (resolver forwards DNS queries from the DNS service for a VPC to your network or another VPC)
resolver-endpoint.name: direction

resolver-endpoint.description: Endpoint IP type. This endpoint type is applied to all IP addresses. Valid values are `IPV6`, `IPV4` or `DUALSTACK` (both IPv4 and IPv6)
resolver-endpoint.name: type

resolver-firewall-rule-group.description: Determines whether resources will be created (affects all resources)
resolver-firewall-rule-group.name: create

resolver-firewall-rule-group.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
resolver-firewall-rule-group.name: region

resolver-firewall-rule-group.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
resolver-firewall-rule-group.name: tags

resolver-firewall-rule-group.description: A name that lets you identify the rule group, to manage and use it
resolver-firewall-rule-group.name: name

resolver-firewall-rule-group.description: A map of RAM resource associations for the created rule group
resolver-firewall-rule-group.name: ram_resource_associations

resolver-firewall-rule-group.description: A map of rule definitions to create
resolver-firewall-rule-group.name: rules
resolver-firewall-rule-group.type:
map(object({
    name = optional(string)

    # Domain list
    domains = optional(list(string))

    # Rule
    action                             = string
    block_override_dns_type            = optional(string)
    block_override_domain              = optional(string)
    block_override_ttl                 = optional(number)
    block_response                     = optional(string)
    firewall_domain_list_id            = optional(string)
    firewall_domain_redirection_action = optional(string)
    priority                           = number
    q_type                             = optional(string)

    tags = optional(map(string), {})
  }))

root.description: The ARN of the KMS key to use for DNSSEC signing. Required when `create_dnssec_kms_key` is `false`
root.name: dnssec_kms_key_arn

root.description: Additional tags to apply to the KMS key created for DNSSEC signing
root.name: dnssec_kms_key_tags

root.description: Configuration block(s) specifying VPC(s) to associate with a private hosted zone. Conflicts with the delegation_set_id argument in this resource and any aws_route53_zone_association resource specifying the same zone ID
root.name: vpc

root.description: A list of aliases to create. Note - due to the use of `toset()`, values must be static strings and not computed values
root.name: dnssec_kms_key_aliases

root.description: Name of the Route 53 key signing key (KSK). When null, the hosted zone name is used. Set this to match an existing KSK when importing or adopting DNSSEC without recreating the key
root.name: dnssec_key_signing_key_name

root.description: Tags added to all zones. Will take precedence over tags from the 'zones' variable
root.name: tags

root.description: Determines whether to create the Route53 zone or lookup an existing zone
root.name: create_zone

root.description: Determines whether to ignore VPC association changes after creation to avoid disruptive diffs when using `aws_route53_zone_association` resource(s). Changing is a destructive action; users should be prepared to use Terraform state move commands/blocks when changing this value
root.name: ignore_vpc

root.description: Whether the hosted zone is private. Only applicable when `create_zone = false`
root.name: private_zone

root.description: A comment for the hosted zone. Defaults to `Managed by Terraform`
root.name: comment

root.description: Whether to create a KMS key for DNSSEC signing
root.name: create_dnssec_kms_key

root.description: Whether to create Route53 zone
root.name: create

root.description: This is the name of the hosted zone
root.name: name

root.description: Whether to enable DNSSEC for the Route53 zone
root.name: enable_dnssec

root.description: The description of the key as viewed in AWS console
root.name: dnssec_kms_key_description

root.description: A map of Route53 records to create in the zone. The key can be used as the subdomain name, or `name` can be used to specify the full name
root.name: records

root.description: Whether to enable Route 53 Accelerated Recovery for the public hosted zone. When enabled, provides a 60-minute RTO for resuming DNS record management if the US East (N. Virginia) Region becomes unavailable. Only applies to public hosted zones
root.name: enable_accelerated_recovery

root.description: Timeouts for the Route53 zone operations
root.name: timeouts

root.description: A map of VPC association authorizations to create for the Route53 zone
root.name: vpc_association_authorizations

root.description: The ID of the VPC associated with the existing hosted zone. Only applicable when `create_zone = false`
root.name: vpc_id

root.description: The ID of the reusable delegation set whose NS records you want to assign to the hosted zone. Conflicts with vpc as delegation sets can only be used for public zones
root.name: delegation_set_id

root.description: Whether to destroy all records (possibly managed outside of Terraform) in the zone when destroying the zone
root.name: force_destroy

delegation-sets.description: Reference name used when the Route53 delegation set has been created
delegation-sets.name: route53_delegation_set_reference_name

delegation-sets.description: Name servers in the Route53 delegation set
delegation-sets.name: route53_delegation_set_name_servers

resolver-endpoint.description: Resolver Endpoint Rules created
resolver-endpoint.name: rules

resolver-endpoint.description: The ID of the Resolver Endpoint
resolver-endpoint.name: id

resolver-endpoint.description: The ARN of the Resolver Endpoint
resolver-endpoint.name: arn

resolver-endpoint.description: The VPC ID used by the Resolver Endpoint
resolver-endpoint.name: host_vpc_id

resolver-endpoint.description: Security Group IDs mapped to Resolver Endpoint
resolver-endpoint.name: security_group_ids

resolver-endpoint.description: Resolver Endpoint IP Addresses
resolver-endpoint.name: ip_addresses

resolver-endpoint.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the security group
resolver-endpoint.name: security_group_arn

resolver-endpoint.description: ID of the security group
resolver-endpoint.name: security_group_id

resolver-firewall-rule-group.description: The ARN (Amazon Resource Name) of the rule group
resolver-firewall-rule-group.name: arn

resolver-firewall-rule-group.description: The ID of the rule group
resolver-firewall-rule-group.name: id

resolver-firewall-rule-group.description: Whether the rule group is shared with other AWS accounts, or was shared with the current account by another AWS account. Sharing is configured through AWS Resource Access Manager (AWS RAM). Valid values: `NOT_SHARED`, `SHARED_BY_ME`, `SHARED_WITH_ME`
resolver-firewall-rule-group.name: share_status

resolver-firewall-rule-group.description: Map of RAM resource associations created and their attributes
resolver-firewall-rule-group.name: ram_resource_associations

resolver-firewall-rule-group.description: Map of all domain lists created and their attributes
resolver-firewall-rule-group.name: domain_lists

resolver-firewall-rule-group.description: Map of all rules created and their attributes
resolver-firewall-rule-group.name: rules

root.description: The region for the key
root.name: dnssec_kms_key_region

root.description: Records created in the Route53 zone
root.name: records

root.description: Name servers of Route53 zone
root.name: name_servers

root.description: The Route 53 name server that created the SOA record.
root.name: primary_name_server

root.description: A cryptographic digest of a DNSKEY resource record (RR). DNSKEY records are used to publish the public key that resolvers can use to verify DNSSEC signatures that are used to secure certain kinds of information provided by the DNS system
root.name: dnssec_signing_key_digest_value

root.description: Name of Route53 zone
root.name: name

root.description: Route 53 Hosted Zone identifier and KMS Key identifier, separated by a comma (`,`)
root.name: dnssec_signing_key_id

root.description: The public key, represented as a Base64 encoding, as required by RFC-4034 Page 5
root.name: dnssec_signing_key_public_key

root.description: The globally unique identifier for the key
root.name: dnssec_kms_key_id

root.description: The IAM resource policy set on the key
root.name: dnssec_kms_key_policy

root.description: Zone ID of Route53 zone
root.name: id

root.description: A string that represents a DNSKEY record
root.name: dnssec_signing_key_dnskey_record

root.description: An integer used to identify the DNSSEC record for the domain name. The process used to calculate the value is described in RFC-4034 Appendix B
root.name: dnssec_signing_key_tag

root.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the key
root.name: dnssec_kms_key_arn

root.description: Zone ARN of Route53 zone
root.name: arn

root.description: A string that represents a delegation signer (DS) record
root.name: dnssec_signing_key_ds_record
